Circuit design tooling is absorbing AI copilots, while lab bring-up and hardware debugging stay hands-on.
AI accelerates literature review and sequence analysis, but wet-lab bench work remains physical.
Routine modeling and computation automate quickly; framing the right problem is the durable skill.
Reporting, coordination, and analysis tasks are highly automatable; judgment and people management less so.
Rules-based reconciliation, entry, and audit prep are among the most automatable white-collar tasks.
Research, modeling, and reporting compress fast; client trust and fiduciary judgment persist.
Hands-on patient care is among the least automatable work; AI mostly assists with documentation.
Scheduling, billing, and claims workflows automate readily; operating a care organization does not.
Assessment scoring and research analysis automate; therapeutic relationships resist substitution.
Policy research and briefing drafts automate; coalition-building and advocacy remain relational.
Drafting and editing are core LLM strengths; distinctive voice and editorial taste are the moat.
Press releases, social copy, and routine coverage are already machine-drafted.
Image generation collapses production design; direction, systems, and brand strategy hold value.
Commercial illustration is heavily exposed; original physical work and provenance are not.
Diagnostics gain AI assistance, but repair is manual, varied, and on-site.
Factory welding is robotized, but field and repair welding in uncontrolled settings is not.
